data.title: Write structural validation tests for onboarding UX improvements (9 criteria)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description

Create tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh with 10 structural test functions that validate the presence of UX improvements in plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md. All tests must fail (RED) on the current unmodified SKILL.md and pass (GREEN) after the Layer 0 stories (b267-a3b9, 4c55-0c7a, 0487-f12b, fe2b-52dd, 4d15-157d) implement their changes.

## Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh
- MODIFY: .test-index (add entry + RED markers for each test function)

## Test Functions (10)

1. test_orientation_message_present
   Asserts SKILL.md contains "one-time" AND at least one of "project-understanding.md", "dso-config.conf", "shim" within an orientation section at the start.
   Covers done definition: (a) onboarding explains itself upfront

2. test_phase_counter_display
   Asserts SKILL.md contains "Phase [0-9]+ of [0-9]+" regex pattern — NOT just the words "Phase" and "of" separately. Tests that a user-visible counter format is explicitly present.
   Covers done definition: (e) phase counter displayed at start of each phase

3. test_all_three_integration_prompts
   Asserts SKILL.md contains "Figma" AND "Confluence" references (in addition to existing Jira which is already present).
   Covers done definition: (b) all three integration prompts appear

4. test_integration_prompts_skippable
   Asserts SKILL.md contains "skip" language within the integration prompt section (not just anywhere). Use line-number proximity check: find "Figma" or "Confluence" lines, then check for "skip" within 15 lines.
   Covers done definition: (b) integrations labeled as skippable

5. test_dependency_scan_before_questions
   Asserts the dependency scan section appears before Phase 2 (questions). Use line number comparison: line of "dependency" scan section < line of "Phase 2" or "Socratic" dialogue section.
   Covers done definition: (f) dependency scan runs before questions

6. test_optional_deps_non_blocking
   Asserts SKILL.md contains "optional" AND ("ast-grep" OR "semgrep") alongside language indicating they are non-blocking (e.g., "never block", "won't block", "optional" in same paragraph).
   Covers done definition: (g) optional deps separate and non-blocking

7. test_file_write_explanations_present
   Asserts SKILL.md contains explanation language before infrastructure file writes — check for "what this file does" or plain-language explanation near "dso-config.conf" and "shim" write steps.
   Covers done definition: (h) plain-language explanations before file writes

8. test_natural_language_preplanning_question
   DUAL assertion: (1) SKILL.md contains natural-language phrasing for preplanning question, AND (2) SKILL.md does NOT contain raw "(true/false)" in the preplanning question context. Use negative grep on the preplanning question line.
   Covers done definition: (i) natural language preplanning question

9. test_credentials_as_env_vars_only
   Asserts SKILL.md references credentials as environment variables (grep for "JIRA_API_TOKEN" or "FIGMA_PAT" or "API_TOKEN" alongside env var language), and does NOT instruct writing these to dso-config.conf.
   Covers done definition: (d) no credentials in config

10. test_shim_executable_instruction
    Asserts SKILL.md contains instruction to verify or set the shim executable ("chmod +x" OR "test -x" near shim installation section).
    Covers done definition: (c) shim present and executable

## Unit Test Exemption

These are structural/schema validation tests per the Behavioral Test Requirement exemption. All tests grep SKILL.md for content patterns — they test the structural boundary of the SKILL.md artifact, not runtime LLM behavior. This exemption is pre-established in test-onboarding-skill.sh ("metadata/schema validation tests per the Behavioral Test Requirement exemption"). Writing a test-of-test would be circular and change-detector-like.

Exemption criteria cited: Unit exemption criterion 1 (pure structural check) and Rule 5 of the Behavioral Testing Standard (instruction-file task scoring).

## .test-index Update

Add entry:
plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md: tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh

Add RED markers for all 10 test functions.

## Given / When / Then

Given the current unmodified plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md,
When bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh is run,
Then all 10 tests fail (RED), confirming UX improvements are not yet present.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh
- [ ] All 10 test functions fail on current (unmodified) SKILL.md
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh 2>&1 | grep -c "FAIL"
- [ ] .test-index entry added mapping plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md to test-onboarding-ux-validation.sh
  Verify: grep -q 'test-onboarding-ux-validation' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] RED markers present in .test-index for test_orientation_message_present
  Verify: grep '\[test_orientation_message_present\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] RED markers present for test_phase_counter_display, test_shim_executable_instruction
  Verify: grep '\[test_phase_counter_display\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0 (RED markers suppress test gate failures)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
